provisions.title: Abolition of certain rules of law
provisions.content: The rules of law known as the Senatusconsultum Velleianum and the Authentica si qua mulier shall as from the commencement of this Act cease to have the force of law.

provisions.title: Repeal of laws
provisions.content: The laws specified in the Schedule are hereby repealed to the extent set out in the third column thereof.

provisions.title: Saving
provisions.content: The provisions of this Act shall not apply in relation to a juristic act performed before the commencement thereof.

provisions.title: Application in South-West Africa
provisions.content: This Act shall apply also in the territory of South-West Africa, including the Eastern Caprivi Zipfel.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: This Act shall be called the Suretyship Amendment Act, 1971. Schedule Laws Repealed [Schedule amended by Act 22 of 2003 ] No. and Year. Title or Subject Matter. Extent of Repeal. Republic Act 13 of 1944 Land Bank Act, 1944 Section 50bis. Act 24 of 1965 Building Societies Act, 1965 Section 68(3). Act 46 of 1965 Land Bank Amendment Act, 1965 Section 10. Natal Law 40 of 1884 To Regulate Renunciations in Bonds Section 2 and Schedules B, C and D. Act 13 of 1909 To amend Law No. 40, 1884 The whole. History of this document 15 May 2004 this version Amended by Bills of Exchange Act, 2003 16 June 1971 Published in South African Government Gazette 3149 Commenced 04 June 1971 Assented to Related documents Is amended by Bills of Exchange Act, 2003 [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About NamibLII NamibLII is a project of the Law Reform and Development Commission managed by the Directorate: Law Reform of the Ministry of Justice of the Republic of Namibia. In terms of the Law Reform and Development Commission Act, 1991 (Act No. 29 of 1991), the Commission is mandated to consolidate or codify any branch of the law or to introduce other measures aimed at making the law more readily accessible. NamibLII is the embodiment of this mandate making annotated statutes, judgments, and gazettes available in a user-friendly format.
